meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Beat Tutorial Hell: Build User Onboarding as a Student
content.seo_description: Break free from tutorial hell. Learn to build expert user onboarding flows using Antigravity and Claude Code. A hands-on guide for student developers.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends the moment you build a functional feature from scratch. By implementing a user onboarding flow, you bridge the gap between passive watching and active engineering. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to iterate your logic, turning abstract concepts into a deployable, portfolio-ready module.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks watching high-definition videos of other people writing code. You know the syntax, you can follow the instructions, but the moment you open a blank IDE, the screen stares back at you like an empty abyss. This is 'tutorial hell,' and it is the primary graveyard for talented student developers who have the potential to build but lack the framework to start.

Today, we stop watching and start building. We are moving beyond the 'to-do list' app and tackling a real-world engineering challenge: sophisticated user onboarding. By integrating Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you will transform from a passive observer into a builder who ships production-grade features.

steps.title: Define the User Journey Map
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the three critical milestones a new user must hit to experience your app's core value. This prevents feature creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a 3-step user onboarding flow that tracks user progress in a Postgres database. Include fields for current_step, completion_status, and last_active_at.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a user onboarding journey map based on my app's core value proposition'

steps.title: Scaffold the Onboarding State Machine
steps.description: Use a state machine to manage the onboarding flow. This ensures users cannot skip steps and keeps your logic clean and testable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a state machine implementation for a user onboarding flow in TypeScript that handles transitions between 'welcome', 'profile_setup', and 'tutorial_complete'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement the state machine logic in src/onboarding/state.ts'

steps.title: Build the Progress Persistence Layer
steps.description: Ensure that if a user refreshes or leaves, their progress is saved. This is the difference between a toy app and a professional SaaS feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer function in Node.js to update the user onboarding progress in the database and return the next required action.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a service to persist onboarding state to the database'

steps.title: Integrate Contextual Tooltips
steps.description: Implement UI triggers that only show when the user is in the correct state, providing guidance without overwhelming them.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React hook that checks the user's onboarding state and conditionally renders a tooltip component for the 'profile_setup' step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add a useOnboarding hook to conditionally render UI components'

steps.title: Finalize and Deploy
steps.description: Refine the flow, add error handling, and deploy your feature to a staging environment for testing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current onboarding code for edge cases, specifically what happens if a user navigates away mid-process, and suggest robust error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'review onboarding code for edge cases and add necessary error handling'

pro_tips: Always build the 'empty state' first; it is the most neglected part of UX and makes your app feel instantly more professional.
Use telemetry to track where users drop off in your onboarding; if they leave at step 2, your friction is too high.
Never hardcode strings; externalize your onboarding copy into a JSON file so you can A/B test language without redeploying.
Implement an 'onboarding skip' button; users hate being trapped in a flow they already understand.
Treat your onboarding code as a separate micro-module so it can be ported to your next project without refactoring.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, implement an analytics dashboard to track conversion rates. Use that data to iterate on your UI/UX, effectively turning a static feature into a growth engine.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop watching and start shipping. Don't let this knowledge sit idle; apply it to your current project and watch your confidence soar. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
